turns.user_message: I used to walk into rooms without a second thought, completely sure of my own voice and presence. Lately, though, I catch myself shrinking back into the shadows before I even speak. It feels like some invisible weight has settled over my shoulders, making every simple interaction exhausting. I keep wondering where that old version of me went, because this hesitant stranger is so hard to recognize. I just want to feel grounded again instead of constantly apologizing for taking up space.

That fleeting confidence I used to carry around has completely vanished, leaving me stuck in this exhausting loop of second-guessing. Every time I try to push forward, I end up tripping over my own doubts and retreating to familiar, safe corners. It is not just a rough patch anymore because this heavy feeling follows me everywhere I go. I look back at old photos and barely recognize the person who used to laugh so easily and trust their own instincts. Now I just brace myself for the next moment I will inevitably fall short of my own standards.

I honestly do not know how to bridge the massive gap between who I was and who I have become. The person I used to be feels like a distant memory, replaced by this hollow shell that constantly apologizes for existing. Every single day is just another reminder that I am fundamentally lacking whatever it takes to be whole again. I try to force myself to act like I still belong, but the crushing weight of my own inadequacy always wins out in the end. I am so tired of pretending I am still capable when everything inside me screams that I am completely broken.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be.
